chapter_summary: David describes God as his shepherd — providing rest, guidance, protection, and blessing. The most beloved psalm in all Scripture.
main_lesson: God is our shepherd. He takes care of us, guides us, and will always be with us.
memory_verse: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want. (Psalm 23:1)
parent_guide: This may be the most memorised passage in all Scripture. Walk through each image with your child: a shepherd leads, feeds, protects. God does all of this for us. v4 is a perfect verse for children who experience fear — they can say it aloud.
application_for_children: When you are scared, lonely, or worried, you can say: 'God is my shepherd. He is with me.'

verses.kjv: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
verses.little_bible: God is my shepherd. He takes care of me and gives me everything I need.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God looks after me like a shepherd looks after sheep.
verses.meaning: A shepherd protects and provides for his sheep. God does the same for us.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for being my shepherd and always taking care of me.
verses.discussion_question: What does a shepherd do for his sheep?
verses.family_discussion: How has God taken care of our family this week?
verses.do_it_today: Say 'God is my shepherd' out loud three times today — in the morning, at lunch, and at night.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a picture of God as your shepherd, with you as one of His sheep.
verses.illustration_prompt: A peaceful green meadow with a shepherd standing tall, staff in hand, surrounded by fluffy white sheep — one small lamb close to his side. Warm golden light.

verses.kjv: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters.
verses.little_bible: God leads me to grassy fields where I can rest, and to calm, still water where I can drink and be at peace.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God gives me a safe place to rest and peaceful water to drink.
verses.meaning: God provides rest and refreshment for those who follow Him — physically and spiritually.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for giving me rest. Help me to trust that You always lead me somewhere good.
verses.discussion_question: What is your favourite place to rest and feel peaceful?
verses.family_discussion: Where do we feel most peaceful as a family? Can we thank God for that place?
verses.do_it_today: Find a quiet spot today. Sit still for one minute and tell God thank you.
verses.action_challenge: At bedtime, ask God to give you peaceful sleep.
verses.illustration_prompt: Lush green meadow with gentle rolling hills, a still reflective pool, and fluffy sheep resting in the sun. A shepherd stands watching. Soft golden-hour light.

verses.kjv: He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name's sake.
verses.little_bible: God refreshes my heart and soul when I am tired or sad. He guides me along the right path because He is good.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God makes me feel better when I am sad. He shows me the right way to go.
verses.meaning: God restores what is broken in us and keeps guiding us toward what is right — not because we deserve it, but because of who He is.
verses.memory_phrase: God shows me the right path.
verses.prayer: God, when I am sad or feel lost, please refresh me and show me the right way to go.
verses.discussion_question: How does God show us the right way to live?
verses.family_discussion: Has there been a time when God helped our family choose the right thing?
verses.do_it_today: When you're not sure what to do today, stop and ask God: 'Show me the right path.'
verses.action_challenge: Think of one 'right path' choice you can make today — at school, with a friend, or at home.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gentle path winding through a sun-dappled forest, a shepherd guiding a small lamb back onto the path. Warm, hopeful light.

verses.kjv: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.little_bible: Even when I walk through the darkest, scariest places, I will not be afraid — because God is with me. His protection and His guidance comfort me.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Even when things are scary, I am not alone. God is right here with me.
verses.meaning: The 'valley of the shadow' means the darkest, most frightening times of life. God is present even there.
verses.memory_phrase: I will not be afraid — God is with me.
verses.prayer: God, when I feel scared or the dark feels too dark, remind me that You are right beside me. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: What are you sometimes scared of? How can remembering God is with you help?
verses.family_discussion: When our family faces hard things, how can we remind each other that God is with us?
verses.do_it_today: If you feel scared today, say out loud: 'God is with me. I will not be afraid.'
verses.action_challenge: Write or draw this verse on a piece of paper and put it somewhere you'll see it when you feel worried.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking through a dark shadowy valley with a warm glowing light beside them — the presence of God represented as a gentle golden glow. A shepherd's staff is visible. Contrast of dark and gold light.

verses.kjv: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil; mine head with oil; my cup runneth over.
verses.little_bible: God prepares a feast for me — even when things around me are hard. He honours me, blesses me, and fills my life so full of good things that it overflows.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God gives me good things — so many good things that they overflow like a cup too full!
verses.meaning: God's provision and honour are so lavish that they spill over — His blessing is not just enough, it is abundant.
verses.memory_phrase: My cup is running over with good things.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for blessing me with so many good things. Help me notice them all.
verses.discussion_question: What good things has God given you that make your heart feel full?
verses.family_discussion: Name five things God has given our family — make a gratitude list together.
verses.do_it_today: Count five good things God has given you today and say thank you for each one.
verses.action_challenge: Make a family 'full cup' list — write 10 blessings you're grateful for and put it on the fridge.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautifully set table overflowing with food and golden light, a cup spilling over with water that catches the light. Warm celebratory atmosphere.

verses.kjv: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever.
verses.little_bible: God's goodness and love will follow me every single day of my life. And one day, I will live with God in His home — forever.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God is good to me every day. One day I will live with God forever.
verses.meaning: God's blessing is not a moment — it is a lifetime of goodness, and it ends in eternity with Him.
verses.memory_phrase: God's goodness follows me every day.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for promising to love me every day of my life and for the home You are preparing for me with You forever.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean that goodness and love will 'follow' you?
verses.family_discussion: If God's goodness follows us every day, how should that change how we face tomorrow?
verses.do_it_today: Go through your day looking for one sign of God's goodness. Share it at dinner.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, talk about what you think it will be like to live with God forever.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two small figures (goodness and mercy represented as gentle glowing angels) following a child along a sunlit path that leads toward a distant glowing light — heaven. Warm, eternal-feeling light.
